And so with every life event that you choose to disclose, you'll know that you got some connection there if people start to send you cures for what ails you if you will. And so like I probably had now have close to a storage bin full of heal your back stuff that all came in immediately after the back injury. I have everything from DVDs to gadgets to pills to potions to letters to you need to go to this website. You need to talk to this guy. You need to go to this doctor. It has accumulated substantially. And so and it's not, I mean some of it is we don't want them to drop dead yet because we want the next seminar. I recognize that and that's from the usual suspects. But I mean most of it is people who are genuinely now concerned like they would be for a friend or a relative which tells you that's the way they perceive this relationship. And again if you don't get that it tells you a lot about how they perceive their relationship with you. So let's start with the principles on page 12. The principle of fascination. So Rohn's point is interested people want to know if it works. Fascinated people want to know how it works. And so people who are truly fascinated with a thing they want to take the thing apart. They want to actually know how the satellite signal gets from here to there. They want to know how a carburetor actually works. And so they got five manuals about carburetors and they take a carburetor apart and they want to be able to put a carburetor back together. And so that's a difference between just kind of being interested in a thing and being fascinated with a thing. Larry King said that you can't enjoy baseball unless you. Terrific just put it on the floor. Thanks Roger. Thanks a lot. So Larry King told me you can't really be a baseball fan unless you're a statistician. And so true baseball fans get all their joy out of sitting there with all the stats and really studying stats. So a casual person comes to the track. They can have a good time picking names and I'm going to bet the gray horse and we're all going to put two dollars on the table and pick a number you know that kind of stuff. But they don't come back very often. The person who comes to the racetrack all the time is interested beyond just what it is into how does this work. And they learn how to handicap races and they have software that they use and they have math formulas that they use and they know the difference between the gates and they know how to study horse body language and they become sort of compulsive about more and more and more inside information about how all that works right. So the same thing now is true about writing to them as a person. People who are casually interested don't have enormous value to you. But people who are fascinated with you have enormous value to you. And so they have to want to know like why you do what you do how you do what you do what makes you tick you know. And so that requires certain disclosures and it requires certain holdbacks certain mystery about your behavior and your process and your thoughts so that they are always trying to find out more. There are like I get a lot of mail that is professed disbelief about my non-use of technology. And when clients come to the house they are always testing the internet thing. You know to the degree that I'll catch them on a break you know over turn it on a computer to see if they can access the damn internet. Or they'll try the where can I plug this in so I can access the internet while you stick it in your ear if you want to. But there's no you know do you have Wi-Fi what's that you know out in the yard. There's a Starbucks for four hours from here you get on route 77 and you had that but it goes on all the time. And I get mail you know and I get people at seminar you must have you just don't tell it you know I don't have a don't use one of those things you know. I get a lot of the how do you get all your stuff done kind of questions. They are fascinated with how you can have this much output right and not have a big staff you know in order to do it. And so there's disclosure and there's hold back disclosure and hold back. But what you're aiming at is this level of fascination with you that these readers of serial fiction have with these fictional characters which by the way gives you the model. It is making yourself a fictional character. Incidentally one of the things most of your questionnaires John's an exception. Robert's an exception and now I'm going to slight somebody because there was one other exception in the room. But the rest of you are sort of almost taking pride in the fact that you don't read any fiction. Big mistake. Big mistake thinking we're in the nonfiction business. No we're in the fiction business. First of all remember that most of your customers never do anything with most of what they buy from you. Therefore their experience is fictional not non-fictional. Well I mean laugh if you want I'm being very serious. Say they're having a fictional experience. They actually believe they're doing something. They are not. But they think they're doing something. They have activity confused with accomplishment. They even have purchasing confused with accomplishment which is good. But you've got to understand that's where they are. So they have the getting of the thing and the playing in the box and the going in and organizing it in their office. They have that confused in their mind with like actually going out and making money or exercising or you know any of that. And that's okay because if they didn't say none of us are going to get rich on the ones who do. There ain't enough of them. So for most of them they are having a fictional experience and we need to be a fictional character to them. And so not reading fiction not only is there anything to be proud of it's not smart. And many of the best copy writers you know what they have in common. First of all of course they have face to face sales experience. Face to face nose to nose to nose to nose sales. The other thing most of them have in common is they're frustrated fiction writers and they read a lot of fiction. So John Carlton like is a science fiction freak. He tries to write science fiction novels. Halbert was a mystery freak and tried to write mystery fiction. And so the good copy writers pay attention to it and you should too. So here's the big so a big question for you is how fascinated are your customers, members, subscribers whatever term you want to use for simplicity sake I'm going to use customers. How fascinated are your customers with you right now? You kind of know what the evidence is so are there conversations with you personal questions philosophical questions or are they restricted to mechanical practical questions. Is your mail like the mail I've described or is the mail all mail that somebody in the office can handle because they're asking for a copy of page 36 of a manual that they lost. How fascinated are they with you? The process starts with what writers call I'm on page 13 now by the way the personal narrative and the personal narrative is very different from a resume of qualifications or credentials and a lot of people in our kinds of businesses try and influence based on resume. And again that's probably the least compelling thing that we have in our bag of tricks. So it is more about this personal narrative this personal storyline if you will. What is this person about what do they symbolically represent and you want at its core a simple thing. And so if you look at.
